title: organization — PHP attribute contracts
description: One attribute. `AsOrganizationListener` marks a service that reacts to Organization lifecycle events (created / reparented / default-changed / archived / …). The organization module scans for this attribute at boot + auto-registers the class with the event dispatcher — no manual EventServiceProvider wiring per listener.

attributes.name: AsOrganizationListener

params.name: events
params.description: Explicit list of event class-short-names the listener handles (e.g. `['OrganizationCreated', 'OrganizationReparented']`). When empty, the discovery scan reads the class's method signatures + auto-wires (`handleOrganizationCreated(OrganizationCreated $e)` binds to that event). Explicit list wins over signature scan.

params.name: queue
params.description: Queue name for asynchronous handling. Null = synchronous. Common values: `notifications` (welcome flows), `notifications-critical` (compliance signals like default-change).

params.name: priority
params.description: Ordering among listeners for the same event. Lower runs first. Reserve `<0` for framework listeners; consumers start at 0.

params.name: afterCommit
params.description: Fire after the write transaction commits (safe against rollback). Rare exceptions to `false` when the listener MUST run in the same transaction (e.g. seeding the default org during `TenantProvisioning`).
